name: Tuo Zaafi and Ayoyo Soup
instructions: Prepare corn and cassava dough into a smooth swallow. Boil ayoyo leaves with dawadawa and spices until slimy then serve with beef stew.

name: Banku and Okro Soup
instructions: Cook fermented dough into banku. Sauté onions and tomatoes then add chopped okro and seafood to simmer into a thick soup.

name: Waakye with Shito and Egg
instructions: Boil beans with sorghum leaves until soft then add rice to cook together. Serve with boiled egg and dark pepper sauce.

name: Oto (Mashed Yam with Egg)
instructions: Boil yam until soft and mash with a little palm oil and onions. Serve with hard-boiled eggs and avocado slices.

name: Etor (Mashed Plantain)
instructions: Boil slightly ripe plantains and mash in an asanka. Stir in roasted peanut paste and a touch of palm oil.

name: Light Soup with Goat Meat
instructions: Steam goat meat with aromatics. Blend boiled tomatoes and garden eggs then strain into the pot to simmer into a thin soup.

name: Brown Rice Jollof with Grilled Chicken
instructions: Cook brown rice in a spicy tomato and onion base. Serve with oven-grilled chicken breast and steamed vegetables.

name: Hausa Koko and Koose
instructions: Boil fermented millet dough into a spicy porridge. Deep fry seasoned cowpea batter into fluffy bean cakes.

name: Kokonte and Groundnut Soup
instructions: Stir dried cassava flour in boiling water until brown and stretchy. Mix peanut paste with water and simmer with chicken and spices.

name: Agushie Stew with Boiled Plantain
instructions: Steam grounded melon seeds into a tomato sauce until chunky. Serve with boiled green or ripe plantains.

name: Fante Fante and Boiled Yam
instructions: Simmer fresh fish in a light palm oil and tomato sauce without much stirring. Serve with sliced boiled yam.

name: Grilled Guinea Fowl with Kachumbari
instructions: Season guinea fowl with local spices and grill over charcoal. Serve with a fresh tomato and onion salad.

name: Bambara Beans (Aboboi) and Plantain
instructions: Boil bambara beans until very soft and slightly sweet. Serve as a mash with boiled or roasted ripe plantain.

name: Sweet Potato and Garden Egg Stew
instructions: Boil sweet potatoes. Sauté onions and peppers, add chopped garden eggs and simmer until soft for a healthy stew.

name: Akyeke with Grilled Tilapia
instructions: Steam fermented cassava pulp (akyeke). Serve with seasoned grilled tilapia, fresh peppers, and avocado slices.

name: Plantain Avocado Sourdough
instructions: Toast bread and mash avocado with lime. Pan-fry plantains without oil. Assemble toast and top with plantains.

name: Kelewele Chickpea Bowl
instructions: Season plantains with ginger and pepper then air-fry. Sauté chickpeas with onions and spices. Mix with fresh spinach in a bowl.

name: Groundnut Sweet Potato Stew
instructions: Boil diced sweet potatoes until tender. Simmer peanut butter and tomato sauce with broth. Add potatoes and greens until thickened.

name: Quinoa Jollof with Tofu
instructions: Cook quinoa in a spiced jollof tomato base. Pan-sear seasoned tofu cubes until crispy. Combine and garnish with peppers.

name: Kenkey and Shrimp Stir-fry
instructions: Dice kenkey into cubes and lightly toast. Stir-fry shrimp with ginger, garlic, and julienned carrots. Toss kenkey cubes into the pan to coat.

name: Millet Berry Breakfast Bowl
instructions: Cook millet until creamy like porridge. Top with a dollop of yogurt and fresh berries. Drizzle with honey before serving.

name: Suya Beef Tacos
instructions: Coat beef strips in suya spice and grill quickly. Warm tortillas and fill with beef. Top with fresh cabbage slaw and onions.

name: Cassava Gnocchi Marinara
instructions: Mix cassava flour and egg to form dough nuggets. Boil until they float, then toss in warm tomato sauce. Garnish with fresh basil.

name: Grilled Grouper with Mango Salsa
instructions: Grill seasoned fish fillet until flaky. Combine diced mango, onions, and lime for salsa. Serve over a bed of steamed brown rice.

name: Brown Rice Waakye and Turkey
instructions: Boil brown rice and beans with waakye leaves for color. Grill turkey breast with minimal oil. Serve with a side of steamed vegetables.

name: Garden Egg Ratatouille
instructions: Slice garden eggs and zucchini into thin rounds. Layer in a dish with spiced tomato reduction and bake. Drizzle with olive oil.

name: Okra and Shrimp Zoodles
instructions: Spiralize zucchini into noodles. Sauté shrimp and chopped okra with garlic and chili. Toss in zoodles for 2 minutes and serve.

name: Poached Eggs on Agege Toast
instructions: Poach eggs in simmering water. Toast a thick slice of bread and top with sautéed spinach. Place eggs on top and season.

name: Yam Mash and Grilled Lobster
instructions: Boil and mash yam with a little milk and garlic. Grill lobster tail with herb butter. Serve mash with lobster and steamed greens.

name: Turkey and Black-eyed Pea Chili
instructions: Brown turkey in a pot with onions. Add pre-soaked beans, tomatoes, and spices. Simmer for 30 minutes until thick.

name: Banku and Okro Stew
instructions: Cook dough into banku. Sauté onions and tomatoes, add chopped okra and protein. Simmer until thickened.

name: Brown Rice Waakye
instructions: Boil beans with waakye leaves until soft. Add rice and cook until tender. Serve with shito and egg.

name: Tuo Zaafi and Ayoyo Soup
instructions: Prepare corn swallow. Boil Ayoyo leaves with ground fish and meat. Combine and serve hot.

name: Omo Tuo and Groundnut Soup
instructions: Cook rice until very soft and mold into balls. Simmer peanut butter with chicken broth and spices.

name: Mpo Tormporm (Yam Porridge)
instructions: Peel and dice yam. Boil with onions, palm oil, and fish until soft and mushy. Mash slightly.

name: Healthy Red Red
instructions: Boil beans until soft. Air-fry plantain slices instead of deep frying. Sauté onions and mix with beans.

name: Etɔ (Mashed Yam with Egg)
instructions: Boil yam and mash with a touch of palm oil. Top with roasted peanuts and hard-boiled eggs.

name: Kokonte and Groundnut Soup
instructions: Stir flour in boiling water to make kokonte. Simmer groundnut paste with fish and spices for the soup.

name: Jollof Rice with Grilled Fish
instructions: Cook rice in a spiced tomato base. Season and grill tilapia separately. Serve with a side of salad.

name: Agushie Stew and Boiled Yam
instructions: Boil yam. Sauté agushie with tomatoes, onions, and spinach. Add fish and simmer until set.

name: Goat Light Soup
instructions: Steam goat meat with spices. Blend vegetables and strain into the pot. Boil until meat is tender.

name: Brown Rice Jollof and Grilled Chicken
instructions: Cook brown rice in tomato and pepper mix. Grill chicken breast with herbs. Serve together.

name: Apapransa with Steamed Crab
instructions: Mix corn flour into hot palm nut soup and stir until thick. Garnish with steamed crab and smoked fish.

name: Hausa Koko and Soya Koose
instructions: Boil water and add millet paste to make porridge. Fry soya bean batter into small, high-protein cakes.

name: Grilled Guinea Fowl and Stir-fry Veggies
instructions: Season and grill guinea fowl until charred and tender. Quickly stir-fry vegetables with minimal oil.

name: Waakye Quinoa Bowl
instructions: Boil quinoa with waakye leaves for color. Stir in pre-cooked beans and serve with shito.

name: Grilled Jerk Chicken with Sweet Potato
instructions: Marinate chicken in jerk spices and grill. Roast sweet potato wedges until tender.

name: Peanut Butter Tofu Stir Fry
instructions: Press tofu and cube it. Stir fry with vegetables and a peanut-soy sauce.

name: Zucchini Noodle Garden Egg Pasta
instructions: Spiralize zucchini into noodles. Sauté garden eggs and garlic then toss with noodles.

name: Millet and Grilled Turkey Salad
instructions: Cook millet until fluffy. Toss with grilled turkey strips and chopped cucumbers.

name: Coconut Poached Red Snapper
instructions: Simmer fish in coconut milk and ginger. Add spinach at the end to wilt.

name: Black-Eyed Pea Hummus Wrap
instructions: Blend beans with tahini to make hummus. Spread on tortilla and wrap with shredded carrots.

name: Fonio Tabbouleh with Grilled Shrimp
instructions: Steam fonio and cool. Mix with herbs and top with grilled seasoned shrimp.

name: Avocado and Plantain Sourdough Toast
instructions: Toast bread and top with mashed avocado. Layer with sliced boiled plantain and chili.

name: Brown Rice Jollof with Tofu
instructions: Cook brown rice in a tomato and onion base. Stir in pan-seared tofu cubes.

name: Curried Okra and Chickpea Stew
instructions: Sauté okra and chickpeas with curry powder. Simmer until the sauce thickens.

name: Ginger-Lime Tilapia with Cauliflower Rice
instructions: Steam fish with ginger and lime. Serve over riced and sautéed cauliflower.

name: Savory Oat Porridge with Spinach and Egg
instructions: Cook oats in water with spinach. Top with a poached or fried egg.

name: Grilled Beef Kofta with Yam Fries
instructions: Shape spiced beef into koftas and grill. Serve with baked yam strips.

name: Lemon Herb Chicken with Steamed Cocoyam
instructions: Marinate chicken in lemon and herbs then roast. Serve with peeled steamed cocoyam.

name: Waakye and Fried Fish
instructions: Boil rice and beans with sorghum leaves until dark purple. Serve with fried fish and shito.

name: Banku and Okro Stew
instructions: Cook banku by stirring dough in hot water. Simmer chopped okro with fish and spices.

name: Quinoa Jollof

name: Grilled Plantain and Avocado Salad
instructions: Grill plantain slices until golden. Mix with diced avocado and tomatoes. Drizzle with lime juice.

name: Chickpea and Yam Curry
instructions: Boil yam cubes until soft. Add chickpeas and coconut curry sauce. Simmer for 10 minutes.

name: Brown Rice and Kontomire Frittata
instructions: Whisk eggs and chopped kontomire. Stir in cooked brown rice. Pan-fry until set and golden.

name: Grilled Turkey and Bulgur
image_url: Grill seasoned turkey breast. Steam bulgur until tender. Serve together with fresh cucumber slices.

name: Millet Porridge with Mango and Seeds
instructions: Boil millet into a smooth porridge. Top with sliced mango and seeds. Sweeten with honey.

name: Tuna and Sweet Potato Mash
instructions: Boil and mash sweet potatoes. Mix in flaked tuna and chopped onions. Season with olive oil.

name: Black-Eyed Pea Hummus Wrap
instructions: Blend cooked peas with tahini. Spread on a wrap and add grated carrots. Roll tightly.

name: Steamed Fish with Ginger and Bok Choy
instructions: Place fish and bok choy in a steamer. Top with ginger and soy. Steam for 10 minutes.

name: Agushie and Mushroom Scramble
instructions: Sauté onions and mushrooms. Add ground agushie paste and scramble until firm and textured.

name: Spicy Oat Fufu with Light Goat Soup
instructions: Prepare fufu from oat flour. Cook goat meat in a spiced tomato broth. Serve together hot.

name: Tofu and Peanut Stew
instructions: Brown tofu cubes. Simmer with peanut butter and tomato sauce. Stir in spinach before serving.

name: Grilled Shrimp and Mango Zoodles
instructions: Spiralize zucchini. Sauté with grilled shrimp and mango slices. Finish with a squeeze of lemon.

name: Fonio Salad with Roasted Veggies
instructions: Steam fonio until fluffy. Toss with roasted peppers and zucchini. Season with herbs and oil.

name: Avocado and Egg Brown Bread Toast
instructions: Toast bread and spread mashed avocado. Top with a poached egg and a sprinkle of chili flakes.

name: Banku and Okro Stew
instructions: Boil chopped okra until sticky. Sauté meat and aromatics in palm oil then combine with okra and serve with fermented dough balls.

name: Tuuo Zaafi and Ayoyo Soup
instructions: Boil ayoyo leaves with dried fish and spices. Prepare a soft millet and cassava dough and serve together with the soup.

name: Waakye with Fried Fish and Wele
instructions: Boil rice and beans together with sorghum leaves for color. Serve with fried fish, spicy shito sauce, and soft cooked cowhide.

name: Omo Tuo and Groundnut Soup
instructions: Cook rice until very soft and mash into firm balls. Simmer peanut butter with a tomato base and chicken until oil rises to the top.

name: Akyeke with Grilled Mackerel
instructions: Steam the cassava couscous (akyeke) until fluffy. Grill seasoned mackerel and serve with a fresh salsa of tomatoes, onions, and chili.
